items.q: Why did the 2010 Haiti earthquake kill so many people compared with similar-magnitude quakes in rich countries?
items.correct: Vulnerability - unreinforced buildings, dense informal housing, poverty and weak emergency services magnified the death toll, not just the magnitude itself.
items.misconception: A bigger death toll always means the earthquake had a bigger magnitude.
items.random_wrong: Haiti's earthquake was magnitude 9.5, the largest ever recorded.
items.why: Comparing Haiti 2010 with stronger quakes in HICs that killed far fewer shows that development level, building quality and response capacity drive impacts.

items.q: Is flowing lava the main killer in volcanic eruptions?
items.correct: No - lava usually moves slowly enough to escape; pyroclastic flows, lahars and ash cause most deaths because they are fast, hot and far-reaching.
items.misconception: Lava flows are the deadliest volcanic hazard.
items.random_wrong: Volcanoes are dangerous mainly because of their loud noise.
items.why: Films exaggerate lava; the 1902 Mt Pelee pyroclastic flow killed around 30,000 in minutes, while lava deaths are comparatively rare.

items.q: Is a magnitude 6 earthquake twice as powerful as a magnitude 3?
items.correct: No - the scale is logarithmic: each whole number is ten times the wave amplitude and roughly 30 times the energy, so a 6 is vastly stronger than a 3.
items.misconception: Magnitude is a linear scale, so 6 is double 3.
items.random_wrong: The Richter scale measures wind speed.
items.why: Students often read magnitude like a percentage; the logarithmic step is why small numerical differences mean huge differences in shaking.
It's Richter. “Mercalli” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The San Andreas Fault is what type of plate margin?
items.why: Correct answer: Conservative. “Constructive” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: At a destructive plate margin, what happens to the denser oceanic plate?
items.correct: It subducts beneath the continental plate
items.misconception: It rides over the continental plate
items.why: The answer examiners want is: It subducts beneath the continental plate. “It rides over the continental plate” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: A fast-moving cloud of hot ash, gas and rock
items.why: Correct answer: A fast-moving cloud of hot ash, gas and rock. “A slow-moving river of lava” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What was the magnitude of the 2010 Haiti earthquake?
items.why: The real figure is 7 Richter. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
